compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are indistinguishable or lost. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable specimen, though severely compromised.

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot; soil appears undisturbed but devoid of plant matter.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains debris (eggshells) but no viable plant tissue.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen shows significant chlorosis and structural collapse; leaf turgor is near zero.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, potentially over-saturated or hydrophobic due to lack of transpiration.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot, likely an amateur attempt at calcium supplementation.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of a white rabbit anchor in P3; no evidence of new growth or secondary seedlings.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a steady decline. The Pothos (P3) has lost structural integrity, transitioning from a semi-upright posture to a prostrate, necrotic state. No recovery observed.

visual_health_inference.P3: Critical. The specimen is likely experiencing root rot or severe light starvation. The lack of turgor suggests a failure in vascular transport.

anomalies: Blue-spectrum light shift in T-1 (Camera/Environment artifact)
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of terminal decline. The Pothos (P3) is the only remaining occupant, exhibiting signs of advanced senescence and dehydration. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) are effectively empty, suggesting either total plant mortality or complete removal. The presence of eggshells indicates a failed intervention. The environment is currently incapable of supporting the registered flora.
